message:
feat(abi): port __cxa_vec_new/new2/new3 + delete/delete2/delete3 + cctor (bd-rdxo4)

Ship the Itanium C++ ABI vector new/delete/copy-ctor family. The
compiler emits these for `new T[n]` / `delete[] arr` over types with
non-trivial constructors/destructors, so missing them blocks linking
of any C++ program that uses array-new.

Layout when padding > 0:

    +----------------- padding -----------------+----- count*size -----+
    | size_t element_count | (rest unused/0)    | T[0] T[1] ... T[n-1] |
    +-------------------------------------------+----------------------+
    ^                                           ^
    `raw` (returned to free)                    `array` (returned to user)

`__cxa_vec_new` stashes `count` at the start of the padding so
`__cxa_vec_delete` can recover it; the Itanium ABI uses padding ==
sizeof(size_t) when the destructor needs the count.

  - __cxa_vec_new(count, size, padding, ctor, dtor): malloc + ctor each
    elem; checked arithmetic; returns NULL on overflow / 0-byte total /
    alloc failure.
  - __cxa_vec_new2/new3: same with caller-supplied alloc_func (and
    dealloc_func ignored at allocation time — only invoked by the
    matching delete2/delete3).
  - __cxa_vec_delete(array, size, padding, dtor): recover count from
    padding, vec_dtor in reverse, free.
  - __cxa_vec_delete2(_, dealloc): use caller dealloc(*c_void).
  - __cxa_vec_delete3(_, dealloc): use caller dealloc(*c_void, size).
  - __cxa_vec_cctor(dest, src, count, size, ctor, dtor): copy-construct
    each element via ctor(d, s) in forward order.

NULL ctor/dtor and NULL array are tolerated (compiler omits the
constructor when the element type is trivially copyable).

Tests: 8 new abi tests covering round-trip new+delete (incl. reverse
dtor visit order and ctor side-effects), overflow returns NULL,
zero-total returns NULL, custom alloc via new2, custom dealloc via
delete2 (verified raw pointer = array - padding), custom dealloc via
delete3 (verified total size = count*size+padding), all-three-deletes
NULL tolerance, and cctor copy-construction with side-effect tracking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
